overview: The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) is a government-backed investment fund that provides funding to startups in India. The scheme is managed by the Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI) and has a corpus of INR 10,000 crores. The fund invests in SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that in turn invest in startups.
objectives: To provide funding to startups in India
To promote entrepreneurship and innovation in the country
To create jobs and stimulate economic growth
eligibility: Startups that are registered in India and meet the eligibility criteria set by the government can apply for funding under the FFS scheme.
benefits: The FFS scheme provides funding to startups, which can be used to develop their business, hire employees, and expand their operations. The scheme also provides mentorship and networking opportunities to startups.
financial_support: The FFS scheme provides funding to startups through SEBI-registered AIFs. The fund invests in AIFs that in turn invest in startups.
application_process: Startups can apply for funding under the FFS scheme through the Startup India website. The application process involves submitting a business plan, financial projections, and other relevant documents.
deadlines: The application process for the FFS scheme is open throughout the year. However, the government may announce specific deadlines for applying to the scheme.
implementing_agency: Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI)
caveats: The FFS scheme is only available to startups that are registered in India.
The scheme has a limited corpus, and funding is provided on a first-come, first-served basis.
